ocr.text: In the current analysis of cap rate compression, we observe the evolving dynamics within North America's real estate market,
focusing on various property types and their respective performance metrics. Notably, the suburban properties, built in 2012,
exhibit market trends categorized as "Stable," indicating a consistent demand in these areas. In contrast, urban residential
properties are experiencing "Increasing" market trends, reflecting a robust interest that may elevate their investment appeal. The
analysis highlights an occupancy rate of 90% for these urban assets, underscoring their desirability and solid performance
